card_description: Spinoza (Portuguese/Hebrew: "From Espinosa (thorny)") is a historical figure appearing in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. In the philosophical tradition, Spinoza is 17th century rationalist; God as Nature (Deus sive Natura). PKD engages with Spinoza's pantheism and substance monism. Appears in 7 Exegesis passages. Associated with dictionary terms: Spinoza. Context: "Dick identifies the Egyptian deity Ptah as the "Immanent Mind" and "Logos" doctrine precursor."

linked_segments.title: U_Claudia_13.txt

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_126.txt

linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_128.txt
